90:   # @param [Class, String, Symbol] adapter

93:   def json=(adapter)
94:     @json = case adapter.to_s
95:     when "JSON", "json" then JSONAdapter::JSON
96:     when "Oj", "oj" then JSONAdapter::Oj
97:     when "Yajl", "yajl" then JSONAdapter::Yajl
98:     else

100:       is_subclass = is_class && adapter.ancestors.include?(LLM::JSONAdapter)

3: ##
4: # The Provider class represents an abstract class for
5: # LLM (Language Model) providers.
6: #
7: # @abstract
8: class LLM::Provider
9:   include LLM::Transport::Execution
10: 
11:   ##
12:   # @param [String, nil] key
13:   #  The secret key for authentication
14:   # @param [String] host
15:   #  The host address of the LLM provider
16:   # @param [Integer] port
17:   #  The port number
18:   # @param [Integer] timeout
19:   #  The number of seconds to wait for a response
20:   # @param [Boolean] ssl
21:   #  Whether to use SSL for the connection
22:   # @param [String] base_path
23:   #  Optional base path prefix for HTTP API routes.
24:   # @param [Boolean] persistent
25:   #  Whether to use a persistent connection.
26:   #  Requires the net-http-persistent gem.
27:   # @param [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] transport
28:   #  Optional override with any {LLM::Transport} instance or subclass.
29:   def initialize(key:, host:, port: 443, timeout: 60, ssl: true, base_path: "", persistent: false, transport: nil)
30:     @key = key
31:     @host = host
32:     @port = port
33:     @timeout = timeout
34:     @ssl = ssl
35:     @base_path = LLM::Utils.normalize_base_path(base_path)
36:     @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
37:     @headers = {"User-Agent" => "llm.rb v#{LLM::VERSION}"}
38:     @transport = LLM::Transport::Utils.resolve_transport(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:, transport:, persistent:)
39:     @monitor = Monitor.new
40:   end
41: 
42:   ##
43:   # Returns an inspection of the provider object
44:   # @return [String]
45:   # @note The secret key is redacted in inspect for security reasons
46:   def inspect
47:     "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @key=[REDACTED] @transport=#{transport.inspect} @tracer=#{tracer.inspect}>"
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
52:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
53:   # @return [Symbol]
54:   #  Returns the provider's name

50:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
51:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
53:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     def embed(input, model: "text-embedding-3-small", **params)
55:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path("/embeddings"), headers)
56:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:, model:}.merge!(params))
57:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
58:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
59:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
60:       res

63:     ##
64:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
65:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI docs
66:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
67:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
68:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
69:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)

73:     def complete(prompt, params = {})

75:       req, messages = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
76:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
77:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
78:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
79:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
80:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
81:       res

176:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
177:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
178:     # @param query [String] The search query.
179:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
180:     def web_search(query:)

191:     end

194:       lock do

197:           "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
198:         )
199:       end
200:     end
201: 
202:     def stream_parser
203:       LLM::OpenAI::StreamParser
204:     end

207:       LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler
208:     end

210:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
211:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
212:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
213:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
214:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
215:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
216:       if params[:stream]
217:         params[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}.merge!(params[:stream_options] || {})
218:       end
219:       [params, stream, tools, role]
220:     end
221: 
222:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
223:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
224:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: adapt(messages, mode: :complete).flatten}.merge!(params))
225:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)
226:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
227:       [req, messages]
228:     end
229: 
230:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
231:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
232:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
233:       else
234:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
235:       end
236:     end

238:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
239:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
240:       value = message&.content || fallback
241:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
242:     end
243:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     EMPTY_HASH = {}.freeze
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
11:     # @return [Hash]
12:     attr_reader :body
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: {}}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
22:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
23:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
24:     end
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [Hash] chunk
28:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
29:     def parse!(chunk)
30:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
31:     end
32: 
33:     ##
34:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
35:     # @return [void]
36:     def free
37:       @emits.clear
38:     end
39: 
40:     private
41: 
42:     def merge!(chunk)
43:       chunk.each do |key, value|
44:         if key == "choices"
45:           @body["choices"] ||= []
46:           merge_choices!(value)
47:         else
48:           @body[key] = value
49:         end
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def merge_choices!(choices)
54:       body_choices = @body["choices"]
55:       choices.each do |choice|
56:         index = choice["index"]
57:         delta = choice["delta"] || EMPTY_HASH
58:         target_message = if body_choice = body_choices[index]
59:           body_choice["message"]
60:         else
61:           body_choices[index] = {"message" => {"role" => "assistant"}}
62:           body_choices[index]["message"]
63:         end
64:         merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
69:       if delta.length == 1
70:         merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
71:       elsif content = delta["content"]
72:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
73:           target_content << content
74:         else
75:           target_message["content"] = content
76:         end
77:         emit_content(content)
78:       elsif reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
79:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
80:           target_reasoning << reasoning
81:         else
82:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
83:         end
84:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
85:       elsif tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
86:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
87:       end
88:       return if delta.length <= 1
89:       delta.each do |key, value|
90:         next if value.nil? || key == "content" || key == "reasoning_content" || key == "tool_calls"
91:         target_message[key] = value
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
96:       if content = delta["content"]
97:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
98:           target_content << content
99:         else
100:           target_message["content"] = content
101:         end
102:         emit_content(content)
103:         return
104:       end
105:       if reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
106:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
107:           target_reasoning << reasoning
108:         else
109:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
110:         end
111:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
112:         return
113:       end
114:       if tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
115:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     def merge_tools!(target, tools)
120:       target["tool_calls"] ||= []
121:       tools.each.with_index do |toola, index|
122:         tindex = toola["index"]
123:         tindex = index unless Integer === tindex && tindex >= 0
124:         toolb = target["tool_calls"][tindex]
125:         functiona = toola["function"]
126:         functionb = toolb && toolb["function"]
127:         if functiona && functionb
128:           # Append to existing function arguments
129:           merge_function!(functionb, functiona)
130:         else
131:           target["tool_calls"][tindex] = toola
132:         end
133:         emit_tool(target["tool_calls"][tindex], tindex)
134:       end
135:     end
136: 
137:     def merge_function!(target, source)
138:       if arguments = source["arguments"]
139:         if target_arguments = target["arguments"]
140:           target_arguments << arguments
141:         else
142:           target["arguments"] = arguments
143:         end
144:       end
145:       if name = source["name"]
146:         if target_name = target["name"]
147:           target_name << name
148:         else
149:           target["name"] = name
150:         end
151:       end
152:       return if source.length <= 2
153:       source.each do |func_key, func_value|
154:         next if func_key == "arguments" || func_key == "name"
155:         target[func_key] ||= +""
156:         target[func_key] << func_value
157:       end
158:     end
159: 
160:     def emit_content(value)
161:       if @can_emit_content
162:         @stream.on_content(value)
163:       elsif @can_push_content
164:         @stream << value
165:       end
166:     end
167: 
168:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
169:       if @can_emit_reasoning_content
170:         @stream.on_reasoning_content(value)
171:       end
172:     end
173: 
174:     def emit_tool(tool, tindex)
175:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
176:       return if @emits[:tools][tindex]
177:       function = tool["function"]
178:       return unless function && tool["id"] && function["name"]
179:       return unless arguments_complete?(function["arguments"])
180:       arguments = parse_arguments(function["arguments"])
181:       return unless arguments
182:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
183:       @emits[:tools][tindex] = true
184:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
188:       registered = @stream.__find__(function["name"])
189:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(function["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
190:         fn.id = tool["id"]
191:         fn.arguments = arguments
192:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
193:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
194:       end
195:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
196:     end
197: 
198:     def parse_arguments(arguments)
199:       return nil if arguments.to_s.empty?
200:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
201:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : nil
202:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
203:       nil
204:     end
205: 
206:     def arguments_complete?(arguments)
207:       value = arguments.to_s.rstrip
208:       !value.empty? && value.end_with?("}")
209:     end
210:   end
211: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.unauthorized?
55:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       elsif res.rate_limited?
57:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
58:       elsif res.not_found?
59:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
60:       else
61:         error = body["error"] || {}
62:         case error["type"]
63:         when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
64:         when "server_error"
65:           LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
66:         else
67:           LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [Exception] error
74:     # @return [LLM::Error]
75:     def handle_invalid_request(error)
76:       case error["code"]
77:       when "context_length_exceeded"
78:         LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
79:       else
80:         LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
86:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
87:     def parse_body!
88:       if String === res.body
89:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
90:       elsif Hash === res.body
91:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
92:       else
93:         res.body
94:       end
95:     rescue
96:       res.body
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Anthropic class implements a provider for
6:   # [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com).
7:   #
8:   # @example
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
14:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
15:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
16:   class Anthropic < Provider
17:     require_relative "anthropic/utils"
18:     require_relative "anthropic/error_handler"
19:     require_relative "anthropic/request_adapter"
20:     require_relative "anthropic/response_adapter"
21:     require_relative "anthropic/stream_parser"
22:     require_relative "anthropic/models"
23:     require_relative "anthropic/files"
24:     include RequestAdapter
25:     extend Utils
26: 
27:     HOST = "api.anthropic.com"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :anthropic
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
44:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages Anthropic docs
45:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
47:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
48:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
49:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
50:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
51:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
52:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
53:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
54:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
55:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
56:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
57:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
58:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
59:       res
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's models API
64:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/models-list
65:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Models]
66:     def models
67:       LLM::Anthropic::Models.new(self)
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's files API
72:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/files Anthropic docs
73:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Files]
74:     def files
75:       LLM::Anthropic::Files.new(self)
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
80:     def assistant_role
81:       "assistant"
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Anthropic expects tool results to be sent as user messages
86:     # containing `tool_result` content blocks rather than a distinct
87:     # `tool` role.
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
89:     def tool_role
90:       :user
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/all-models#model-comparison-table claude-sonnet-4-20250514
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # @note
103:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
104:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
105:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
106:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/web-search-tool Anthropic docs
107:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
108:     def server_tools
109:       {
110:         bash: server_tool(:bash, type: "bash_20250124"),
111:         web_search: server_tool(:web_search, type: "web_search_20250305", max_uses: 5),
112:         text_editor: server_tool(:str_replace_based_edit_tool, type: "text_editor_20250728", max_characters: 10_000)
113:       }
114:     end
115: 
116:     ##
117:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
118:     # Anthropic web search tool.
119:     # @example
120:     #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
121:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
122:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
123:     # @param query [String] The search query.
124:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
125:     def web_search(query:)
126:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]), type: :web_search)
127:     end
128: 
129:     private
130: 
131:     def headers
132:       lock do
133:         (@headers || {}).merge(
134:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
135:           "x-api-key" => @key,
136:           "anthropic-version" => "2023-06-01",
137:           "anthropic-beta" => "files-api-2025-04-14"
138:         )
139:       end
140:     end
141: 
142:     def stream_parser
143:       LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser
144:     end
145: 
146:     def error_handler
147:       LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler
148:     end
149: 
150:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
151:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 1024}.merge!(params)
152:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
153:       params = [params, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
154:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
155:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
156:       [params, stream, tools, role]
157:     end
158: 
159:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
160:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
161:       payload = adapt(messages)
162:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload.merge!(params))
163:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/messages", headers)
164:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
165:       req
166:     end
167: 
168:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
169:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
170:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
171:       else
172:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
173:       end
174:     end
175:   end
176: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::Transport LLM::Transport} class defines the execution
6:   # interface used by {LLM::Provider}.
7:   #
8:   # Custom transports can subclass this class and override {#request} to
9:   # execute provider requests without changing request adapters or
10:   # response adapters.
11:   #
12:   # Providers should construct {LLM::Transport::Request} objects before
13:   # delegating to a transport. Custom transports can execute those
14:   # requests directly, or transform them into backend-specific request
15:   # objects before execution.
16:   #
17:   # Only {#request} is required. The remaining methods are optional hooks
18:   # for features such as interruption, request ownership, or persistence,
19:   # and only need to be implemented when the underlying adapter can
20:   # support them.
21:   #
22:   # Returned responses should implement the
23:   # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} interface. In
24:   # practice this can mean adapting another client's response object so
25:   # existing provider execution, response adapters, and error handlers
26:   # can rely on one normalized response contract instead of
27:   # transport-specific classes.
28:   class Transport
29:     require_relative "transport/request"
30:     require_relative "transport/response"
31:     require_relative "transport/utils"
32:     require_relative "transport/stream_decoder"
33:     require_relative "transport/net_http_adapter"
34:     require_relative "transport/http"
35:     require_relative "transport/persistent_http"
36:     require_relative "transport/curb"
37:     require_relative "transport/execution"
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP transport class.
41:     # @return [Class]
42:     def self.net_http
43:       HTTP
44:     end
45: 
46:     ##
47:     # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP::Persistent transport class.
48:     # @return [Class]
49:     def self.net_http_persistent
50:       PersistentHTTP
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # Returns the optional libcurl (curb) transport class.
55:     # Requires the `curb` gem.
56:     # @return [Class]
57:     def self.curb
58:       Curb
59:     end
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # Performs a request through the transport.
63:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
64:     # @param [Object] owner
65:     # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream

3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
6:   #
7:   # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
8:   # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
9:   #
10:   # @api private
11:   module Execution
12:     private
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Executes a HTTP request
16:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
17:     #  The request to send
18:     # @param [Proc] b
19:     #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
20:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
21:     #  The response from the server
22:     # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
23:     #  When authentication fails
24:     # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
25:     #  When the rate limit is exceeded
26:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
27:     #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
28:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
29:     #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
30:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
31:     def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
32:       stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
33:       owner = transport.request_owner
34:       tracer = self.tracer
35:       span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
36:       res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
37:       res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
38:       [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
39:     rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
40:       raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
41:       raise
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # Handles the response from a request
46:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
47:     #  The response to handle
48:     # @param [Object, nil] span
49:     #  The span
50:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
51:     def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
52:       res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
53:       res
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Parse a HTTP response
58:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
59:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
60:     def parse_response(res)
61:       case res["content-type"]
62:       when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
63:       else res.body
64:       end
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
5: # provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
6: # capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
7: # stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
8: # and shipped with llm.rb.
9: class LLM::Registry
10:   @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @raise [LLM::Error]
14:   #  Might raise an error
15:   # @param [Symbol]
16:   #  A provider name
17:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
18:   def self.for(name)
19:     path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
20:     if File.file?(path)
21:       new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
22:     else
23:       raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   ##
28:   # @param [Hash] blob
29:   #  A model registry
30:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
31:   def initialize(blob)
32:     @registry = LLM::Object.from(blob)
33:     @models = @registry.models
34:   end
35: 
36:   ##
37:   # @return [LLM::Object]
38:   #  Returns model costs
39:   def cost(model:)
40:     lookup(model:).cost
41:   end
42: 
43:   ##
44:   # @return [LLM::Object]
45:   #  Returns model modalities
46:   def modalities(model:)
47:     lookup(model:).modalities
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @return [LLM::Object]
52:   #  Returns model limits such as the context window size
53:   def limit(model:)
54:     lookup(model:).limit
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def lookup(model:)
60:     if @models.key?(model)
61:       @models[model]
62:     else
63:       patterns = {/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ => "", /\A(gpt-.*)-\d{4}$/ => "\\1"}
64:       fallback = find_map(patterns) { model.dup.sub!(_1, _2) } || "none"
65:       if @models.key?(fallback)
66:         @models[fallback]
67:       else
68:         raise LLM::NoSuchModelError, "no such model: #{model} (fallback: #{fallback})"
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   ##
74:   # Similar to #{find} but returns the block's return value
75:   # @return [Object, nil]
76:   def find_map(pair)
77:     result = nil
78:     pair.each_pair { break if result = yield(_1, _2) }
79:     result
80:   end
81: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Test
4:   class Runtime
5:     attr_reader :messages, :usage
6: 
7:     def initialize
8:       @messages = []
9:       @usage = LLM::Object.from(input_tokens: 0, output_tokens: 0, total_tokens: 0)
10:       @talk_result = Object.new
11:       @ask_result = Object.new
12:     end
13: 
14:     def talk(message)
15:       @messages << LLM::Message.new("user", message)
16:       @talk_result
17:     end
18: 
19:     def ask(message)
20:       @messages << LLM::Message.new("user", message)
21:       @ask_result
22:     end
23: 
24:     def talk_result
25:       @talk_result
26:     end
27: 
28:     def ask_result
29:       @ask_result
30:     end
31:   end
32: 
33:   module Harness
34:     module_function
35: 
36:     def postgres_url
37:       ENV["LLMRB_POSTGRESQL_URL"]
38:     end
39: 
40:     def postgres_available?
41:       postgres_unavailable_reason.nil?
42:     end
43: 
44:     def postgres_unavailable_reason
45:       return "LLMRB_POSTGRESQL_URL is not configured" unless postgres_url
46:       require "pg"
47:       conn = ::PG.connect(postgres_url)
48:       conn.close
49:       nil
50:     rescue LoadError
51:       "pg is not installed"
52:     rescue ::PG::Error => ex
53:       "PostgreSQL is unavailable: #{ex.message.lines.first.strip}"
54:     rescue StandardError => ex
55:       "PostgreSQL is unavailable: #{ex.message}"
56:     end
57: 
58:     def active_record_base(adapter = :sqlite)
59:       const_name = {
60:         sqlite: :ActiveRecordSQLiteBase,
61:         postgres: :ActiveRecordPostgresBase
62:       }.fetch(adapter) { raise ArgumentError, "Unknown ActiveRecord adapter: #{adapter.inspect}" }
63:       return const_get(const_name) if const_defined?(const_name, false)
64:       klass = const_set(const_name, Class.new(::ActiveRecord::Base))
65:       klass.abstract_class = true
66:       case adapter
67:       when :sqlite
68:         klass.establish_connection(adapter: "sqlite3", database: ":memory:")
69:       when :postgres
70:         klass.establish_connection(postgres_url)
71:       end
72:       klass
73:     end
74: 
75:     def active_record_connection(adapter = :sqlite)
76:       active_record_base(adapter).connection
77:     end
78: 
79:     def create_active_record_table(name, adapter: :sqlite, jsonb: false)
80:       conn = active_record_connection(adapter)
81:       return if conn.data_source_exists?(name)
82:       conn.create_table(name) do |t|
83:         t.string :provider
84:         t.string :model
85:         jsonb ? t.jsonb(:data) : t.text(:data)
86:         t.integer :input_tokens
87:         t.integer :output_tokens
88:         t.integer :total_tokens
89:       end
90:     end
91: 
92:     def build_active_record_model(name, adapter: :sqlite, jsonb: false, &block)
93:       create_active_record_table(name, adapter:, jsonb:)
94:       Class.new(active_record_base(adapter)) do
95:         self.table_name = name.to_s
96:         class_eval(&block) if block
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     def sequel_db(adapter = :sqlite)
101:       @sequel_dbs ||= {}
102:       @sequel_dbs[adapter] ||= case adapter
103:       when :sqlite then ::Sequel.sqlite
104:       when :postgres then ::Sequel.connect(postgres_url)
105:       else raise ArgumentError, "Unknown Sequel adapter: #{adapter.inspect}"
106:       end
107:     end
108: 
109:     def create_sequel_table(name, adapter: :sqlite, jsonb: false)
110:       db = sequel_db(adapter)
111:       return if db.table_exists?(name)
112:       db.create_table(name) do
113:         primary_key :id
114:         String :provider
115:         String :model
116:         if jsonb
117:           column :data, "jsonb"
118:         else
119:           String :data, text: true
120:         end
121:         Integer :input_tokens
122:         Integer :output_tokens
123:         Integer :total_tokens
124:       end
125:     end
126: 
127:     def build_sequel_model(name, adapter: :sqlite, jsonb: false, &block)
128:       create_sequel_table(name, adapter:, jsonb:)
129:       Class.new(::Sequel::Model(sequel_db(adapter)[name])) do
130:         class_eval(&block) if block
131:       end
132:     end
133:   end
134: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Google class implements a provider for
6:   # [Gemini](https://ai.google.dev/). The Google provider
7:   # can accept multiple inputs (text, images, audio, and video).
8:   # The inputs can be provided inline via the prompt for files
9:   # under 20MB or via the Gemini Files API for files
10:   # that are over 20MB.
11:   #
12:   # @example
13:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
14:   #   require "llm"
15:   #
16:   #   llm = LLM.google(key: ENV["KEY"])
17:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
18:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
19:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
6:   # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
7:   #
8:   # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
9:   # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
10:   # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
11:   # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
12:   #
13:   # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
14:   # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
15:   # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
16:   # instead of a single API key.
17:   #
18:   # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
19:   # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
20:   # Ruby's stdlib.
21:   #
22:   # @example
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
26:   #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
27:   #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
28:   #     region: "us-east-1"
29:   #   )
30:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
31:   #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
32:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }

35:     require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"

50:     # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
51:     # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
52:     # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
53:     # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
54:     # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
55:     # @param [Integer] port Connection port
56:     # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
57:     # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds

60:                    host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,

184:         access_key_id: @access_key_id,
185:         secret_access_key: @secret_access_key,
186:         aws_region: @aws_region,
187:         host: @aws_host,
188:         session_token: @session_token
189:       )
190:     end

193:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser
194:     end

197:       LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler
198:     end

202:     end

204:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
205:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
206:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
207:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
208:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
209:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
210:       [params, stream, tools, role]
211:     end
212: 
213:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
214:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
215:       model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
216:       payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
217:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
218:       path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
219:                     : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
220:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
221:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
222:       [req, messages, body]
223:     end
224: 
225:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
226:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
227:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
228:       else
229:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
230:       end
231:     end
232: 
233:     def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
234:       adapted = adapt(messages)
235:       payload = {}
236:       payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
237:       payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
238:       inference_config = {}
239:       inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
240:       inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
241:       inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
242:       inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
243:       payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
244:       payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
245:       payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
246:       additional = {}
247:       top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
248:       additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
249:       payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
250:       payload
251:     end

253:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
254:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
255:       value = message&.content || fallback
256:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)

259:     def model_id_for(path)
260:       path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model

263:     def sign!(req, body)
264:       Signature.new(
265:         credentials:,
266:         method: req.method,
267:         path: req.path,
268:         body:
269:       ).sign!(req)
270:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "digest"
4: 
5: class LLM::Bedrock
6:   ##
7:   # Decodes AWS Event Stream binary frames.
8:   #
9:   # Bedrock Converse Stream uses the AWS Event Stream protocol,
10:   # a binary framing format (not SSE). Each message has:
11:   #   - total length (4 bytes, big-endian)
12:   #   - headers length (4 bytes, big-endian)
13:   #   - prelude CRC (4 bytes)
14:   #   - headers (variable)
15:   #   - payload (variable, usually JSON)
16:   #   - message CRC (4 bytes)
17:   #
18:   # Implements #<< to match the interface expected by llm.rb's
19:   # streaming transport, so it can replace the SSE-based
20:   # StreamDecoder when streaming from Bedrock.
21:   #
22:   # @api private
23:   class StreamDecoder
24:     ##
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser]
26:     attr_reader :parser
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @param [LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser] parser
30:     def initialize(parser)
31:       @buffer = +"".b
32:       @parser = parser
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # Feeds a raw binary chunk into the decoder.
37:     # Accumulates data until complete frames are available,
38:     # then decodes them and passes the JSON payload to the parser.
39:     #
40:     # @param [String] chunk Raw binary data
41:     # @return [void]
42:     def <<(chunk)
43:       @buffer << chunk
44:       decode_frames
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # @return [Hash] The fully constructed response body
49:     def body
50:       parser.body
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # @return [void]
55:     def free
56:       @buffer.clear
57:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
58:     end
59: 
60:     private
61: 
62:     def decode_frames
63:       loop do
64:         break if @buffer.bytesize < 12
65:         total_length = @buffer[0, 4].unpack1("N")
66:         break if @buffer.bytesize < total_length
67:         # headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
68:         # prelude_crc = @buffer[8, 4].unpack1("N")
69:         headers = decode_headers
70:         payload_start = 12 + headers[:length]
71:         payload_length = total_length - payload_start - 4
72:         payload = @buffer[payload_start, payload_length] if payload_length > 0
73:         # message_crc from last 4 bytes, not needed for our purposes
74:         json = payload ? LLM.json.load(payload) : {}
75:         parser.parse!(json, event_type: headers[:event_type]) if json.is_a?(Hash)
76:         @buffer = @buffer[total_length..] || +"".b
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode_headers
81:       headers_length = @buffer[4, 4].unpack1("N")
82:       offset = 12
83:       end_offset = offset + headers_length
84:       result = {event_type: nil, length: headers_length}
85:       while offset < end_offset
86:         name_len = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
87:         offset += 1
88:         break if offset + name_len > end_offset
89:         name = @buffer[offset, name_len]
90:         offset += name_len
91:         break if offset >= end_offset
92:         value_type = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
93:         offset += 1
94:         value = case value_type
95:         when 7 # string
96:           str_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
97:           offset += 2
98:           str = @buffer[offset, str_len]
99:           offset += str_len
100:           str
101:         when 8 # binary
102:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
103:           offset += 2
104:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
105:           offset += bin_len
106:           bin
107:         when 9 # boolean true
108:           true
109:         when 1 # boolean false
110:           false
111:         when 2 # byte
112:           val = @buffer.getbyte(offset)
113:           offset += 1
114:           val
115:         when 3 # int16
116:           val = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("s>")
117:           offset += 2
118:           val
119:         when 4 # int32
120:           val = @buffer[offset, 4].unpack1("l>")
121:           offset += 4
122:           val
123:         when 6 # byte array (as raw string)
124:           bin_len = @buffer[offset, 2].unpack1("n")
125:           offset += 2
126:           bin = @buffer[offset, bin_len]
127:           offset += bin_len
128:           bin
129:         else
130:           # Unknown type, skip to end of headers
131:           offset = end_offset
132:           nil
133:         end
134:         result[:event_type] = value if name == ":event-type"
135:         result[name] = value if name
136:       end
137:       result
138:     end
139:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Parses Bedrock Converse Stream events into a response body
6:   # and emits stream callbacks (on_content, on_tool_call, etc.).
7:   #
8:   # Receives decoded JSON payloads from {StreamDecoder} along with
9:   # the AWS Event Stream event type header.
10:   #
11:   # Bedrock Converse Stream event types:
12:   #   messageStart — initial role
13:   #   contentBlockStart — tool use or reasoning start
14:   #   contentBlockDelta — text delta, tool input JSON, or reasoning text
15:   #   contentBlockStop — content block finished
16:   #   messageStop — final stop reason, usage metadata
17:   #
18:   # @api private
19:   class StreamParser
20:     TOOL_MARKER = "<｜DSML｜function_calls"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # @return [Hash] Fully constructed response body
24:     attr_reader :body
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
28:     def initialize(stream)
29:       @body = {"output" => {"message" => {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}}}
30:       @stream = stream
31:       @text_markers = {}
32:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
33:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
34:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
35:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
36:     end
37: 
38:     ##
39:     # @param [Hash] payload Decoded JSON from an event stream frame
40:     # @param [String, nil] event_type The :event-type header value
41:     # @return [self]
42:     def parse!(payload, event_type: nil)
43:       type = event_type || payload["type"]
44:       case type
45:       when "messageStart"
46:         # { "role" => "assistant" }
47:       when "contentBlockStart"
48:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "start" => { "toolUse" => {...} } }
49:         handle_content_block_start(payload)
50:       when "contentBlockDelta"
51:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "delta" => { "text" => "..." } }
52:         handle_content_block_delta(payload)
53:       when "contentBlockStop"
54:         handle_content_block_stop(payload)
55:       when "messageStop"
56:         # { "stopReason" => "end_turn", "metadata" => {"usage" => {...}} }
57:         merge_metadata(payload)
58:       when "metadata"
59:         # { "usage" => {...} }
60:         merge_metadata(payload)
61:       end
62:       self
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # @return [void]
67:     def free
68:       @text_markers.clear
69:     end
70: 
71:     private
72: 
73:     def handle_content_block_start(payload)
74:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
75:       start_data = payload["start"] || {}
76:       if (tool_use = start_data["toolUse"])
77:         content[index] = {"toolUse" => {"toolUseId" => tool_use["toolUseId"], "name" => tool_use["name"], "input" => +""}}
78:       elsif (reasoning = start_data["reasoningContent"])
79:         content[index] = {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +"", "signature" => reasoning["signature"]}.compact}
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def handle_content_block_delta(payload)
84:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
85:       delta = payload["delta"] || {}
86:       if (text = delta["text"])
87:         ensure_content_block(index, "text")
88:         visible = filtered_text(index, text)
89:         return if visible.empty?
90:         content[index]["text"] ||= +""
91:         content[index]["text"] << visible
92:         emit_content(visible)
93:       elsif (tool_input = delta.dig("toolUse", "input"))
94:         ensure_content_block(index, "tool_use")
95:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] ||= +""
96:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] << tool_input
97:       elsif (reasoning = delta["reasoningContent"])
98:         ensure_content_block(index, "reasoning")
99:         if reasoning["text"]
100:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] ||= +""
101:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] << reasoning["text"]
102:           emit_reasoning_content(reasoning["text"])
103:         end
104:         if reasoning["signature"]
105:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["signature"] = reasoning["signature"]
106:         end
107:       end
108:     end
109: 
110:     def handle_content_block_stop(payload)
111:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
112:       item = content[index]
113:       return unless item
114:       flush_text(index, item)
115:       if item["toolUse"] && item["toolUse"]["input"].is_a?(String)
116:         parsed = LLM.json.load(item["toolUse"]["input"])
117:         item["toolUse"]["input"] = parsed.is_a?(Hash) ? parsed : {}
118:         emit_tool(item)
119:       end
120:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
121:       item["toolUse"]["input"] = {} if item&.dig("toolUse")
122:     end
123: 
124:     def ensure_content_block(index, type)
125:       content[index] ||= case type
126:       when "tool_use" then {"toolUse" => {"input" => +""}}
127:       when "reasoning" then {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +""}}
128:       else {}
129:       end
130:     end
131: 
132:     def filtered_text(index, text)
133:       state = (@text_markers[index] ||= +"")
134:       value = state << text
135:       value.gsub!(TOOL_MARKER, "")
136:       keep = marker_prefix_length(value)
137:       @text_markers[index] = keep.zero? ? +"" : value[-keep..]
138:       keep.zero? ? value : value[0...-keep]
139:     end
140: 
141:     def flush_text(index, item)
142:       value = @text_markers.delete(index).to_s
143:       return unless item["text"]
144:       if value.empty?
145:         content[index] = {} if item["text"].empty?
146:       else
147:         item["text"] << value
148:         emit_content(value)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def marker_prefix_length(value)
153:       [value.length, TOOL_MARKER.length - 1].min.downto(1) do |length|
154:         return length if TOOL_MARKER.start_with?(value[-length..])
155:       end
156:       0
157:     end
158: 
159:     def merge_metadata(payload)
160:       metadata = payload["metadata"] || payload
161:       return unless metadata.is_a?(Hash)
162:       usage = metadata["usage"]
163:       @body["usage"] = usage if usage
164:       @body["stopReason"] = payload["stopReason"] if payload["stopReason"]
165:     end
166: 
167:     def emit_content(value)
168:       if @can_emit_content
169:         @stream.on_content(value)
170:       elsif @can_push_content
171:         @stream << value
172:       end
173:     end
174: 
175:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
176:       @stream.on_reasoning_content(value) if @can_emit_reasoning_content
177:     end
178: 
179:     def emit_tool(tool)
180:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
181:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
182:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
183:     end
184: 
185:     def resolve_tool(tool)
186:       payload = tool["toolUse"] || {}
187:       registered = @stream.__find__(payload["name"])
188:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(payload["name"])).dup.tap do |f|
189:         f.id = payload["toolUseId"]
190:         f.arguments = payload["input"] || {}
191:         f.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
192:         f.model = @stream.extra[:model]
193:       end
194:       [fn, registered ? nil : fn.unavailable]
195:     end
196: 
197:     def content
198:       @body["output"]["message"]["content"]
199:     end
200:   end
201: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Handles Bedrock API error responses.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock errors come as JSON with:
8:   #   { "message" => "...", "__type" => "..." }
9:   # or as standard HTTP status codes.
10:   #
11:   # @api private
12:   class ErrorHandler
13:     ##
14:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
15:     attr_reader :res
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @return [Object, nil]
19:     attr_reader :span
20: 
21:     ##
22:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
23:     # @param [Object, nil] span
24:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler]
26:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
27:       @tracer = tracer
28:       @span = span
29:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [LLM::Error]
45:     def error
46:       message = extract_message
47:       if res.server_error?
48:         LLM::ServerError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
49:       elsif res.unauthorized?
50:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
51:       elsif res.forbidden?
52:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
53:       elsif res.rate_limited?
54:         LLM::RateLimitError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
55:       elsif res.not_found?
56:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
57:       else
58:         LLM::Error.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # @return [String]
64:     def extract_message
65:       body = parse_body
66:       body["message"] || body["Message"] || body["__type"] || "Unexpected error"
67:     end
68: 
69:     ##
70:     # @return [Hash]
71:     def parse_body
72:       return {} if res.body.nil? || res.body.empty?
73:       parsed = LLM.json.load(res.body.dup.force_encoding(Encoding::UTF_8).scrub)
74:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
75:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
76:       {}
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
6:   # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
7:   # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
8:   # that can process both images and text.
9:   #
10:   # @example
11:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
12:   #   require "llm"
13:   #
14:   #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
15:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
16:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
17:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
18:   class Ollama < Provider
19:     require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
20:     require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
21:     require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
22:     require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
23:     require_relative "ollama/models"
24: 
25:     include RequestAdapter
26: 
27:     HOST = "localhost"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :ollama
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an embedding
44:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
45:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
47:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
48:     # @return [LLM::Response]
49:     def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
50:       params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
51:       req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
52:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
53:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
54:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
55:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
56:       res
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
61:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
62:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
63:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
64:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
65:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
66:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
67:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
68:     # @return [LLM::Response]
69:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
70:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
71:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
72:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
73:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
74:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
75:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
76:       res
77:     end
78: 
79:     ##
80:     # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
81:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
82:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
83:     def models
84:       LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
89:     def assistant_role
90:       "assistant"
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "qwen3:latest"
99:     end
100: 
101:     private
102: 
103:     def headers
104:       lock do
105:         (@headers || {}).merge(
106:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
107:           "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
108:         )
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     def stream_parser
113:       LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
114:     end
115: 
116:     def error_handler
117:       LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
118:     end
119: 
120:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
121:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
122:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
123:       params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
124:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
125:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
126:       [params, stream, tools, role]
127:     end
128: 
129:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
130:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
131:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
132:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
133:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
134:       req
135:     end
136: 
137:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
138:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
139:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
140:       else
141:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
142:       end
143:     end
144:   end
145: end

13:     # @return [Object, nil]

16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error

### Step 1: Orient in the codebase

This project is `llm.rb`, a Ruby library for interacting with various LLM providers. The codebase is organized as follows:

- **Core library**: `lib/llm.rb` defines the main module and entry points for each provider.
- **Provider implementations**: Each provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) is implemented in `lib/llm/providers/<provider>.rb`.
- **Shared machinery**: `lib/llm/provider.rb` defines the base `LLM::Provider` class that all providers inherit from.
- **Transport layer**: `lib/llm/transport.rb` and related files handle HTTP requests and responses.
- **Registry**: `lib/llm/registry.rb` manages model metadata and capabilities.
- **Error handling**: `lib/llm/error.rb` defines error types used across providers.

Integrations with external LLM providers live in the `lib/llm/providers/` directory. Each provider is a subclass of `LLM::Provider` and implements methods like `complete`, `embed`, and `chat`. Callers obtain a specific provider by calling methods like `LLM.openai`, `LLM.anthropic`, etc., defined in `lib/llm.rb`.

### Step 2: Trace a chat call from the entry point to request and response handling

1. **Entry point**: A caller obtains a provider instance via `LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])` in `lib/llm.rb:152`.
2. **Provider initialization**: The `LLM::OpenAI` class is initialized in `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:33`.
3. **Chat completion**: The `complete` method is called in `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:73`.
4. **Request building**: The `build_complete_request` method constructs the request in `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:222`.
5. **Request execution**: The `execute` method in `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:31` handles the request execution.
6. **Response handling**: The `handle_response` method in `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:51` processes the response.
7. **Response adaptation**: The `ResponseAdapter.adapt` method in `lib/llm/providers/openai/response_adapter.rb:24` adapts the response to the library's shape.

### Step 3: Map the provider family and its base contract

The shared contract for providers is defined in `lib/llm/provider.rb`. Each provider must implement:

- `name`: Returns the provider's name (e.g., `:openai`, `:anthropic`).
- `complete`: Handles chat completions.
- `embed`: Handles embeddings.
- `responses`: Returns an interface to the responses API.
- `images`: Returns an interface to the images API.
- `audio`: Returns an interface to the audio API.
- `files`: Returns an interface to the files API.
- `moderations`: Returns an interface to the moderations API.
- `vector_stores`: Returns an interface to the vector stores API.
- `assistant_role`: Returns the role of the assistant.
- `default_model`: Returns the default model for chat completions.

Existing providers customize this contract as follows:

- **OpenAI**: Implements all methods and includes additional features like `web_search`.
- **Anthropic**: Implements `complete`, `models`, `files`, and customizes `tool_role`.
- **Google**: Implements `complete`, `embed`, `audio`, `images`, `files`, `models`, and customizes roles.
- **Bedrock**: Implements `complete` and `models`, but raises `NotImplementedError` for other methods.
- **LlamaCpp**: Inherits from `OpenAI` but raises `NotImplementedError` for unsupported methods.
- **Ollama**: Implements `complete`, `embed`, and `models`.
- **DeepSeek**: Inherits from `OpenAI` but raises `NotImplementedError` for unsupported methods.
- **XAI**: Inherits from `OpenAI` and customizes `images`.
- **ZAI**: Inherits from `OpenAI` and customizes the `completions_path`.

### Step 4: Map the per-provider component families

Each provider owns a set of components:

- **Request Adapter**: Builds outgoing requests (e.g., `lib/llm/providers/openai/request_adapter.rb`).
- **Response Adapter**: Parses responses into the library's shape (e.g., `lib/llm/providers/openai/response_adapter.rb`).
- **Stream Parser**: Handles streaming responses (e.g., `lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb`).
- **Error Handler**: Maps HTTP failures to typed errors (e.g., `lib/llm/providers/openai/error_handler.rb`).

### Step 5: Map the streaming and error-handling paths

- **Streaming**: Each provider's `stream_parser` class handles incremental parsing of streamed responses. For example, `LLM::OpenAI::StreamParser` in `lib/llm/providers/openai/stream_parser.rb`.
- **Error Handling**: Each provider's `error_handler` class maps HTTP failures to typed errors. For example, `LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler` in `lib/llm/providers/openai/error_handler.rb`.

### Step 6: Find every site that must change to add a new provider

To add a new provider, the following files must be modified or created:

1. **Provider class**: Create a new file in `lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>.rb`.
2. **Entry point**: Add a method in `lib/llm.rb` to instantiate the new provider.
3. **Request Adapter**: Create a request adapter in `lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/request_adapter.rb`.
4. **Response Adapter**: Create a response adapter in `lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/response_adapter.rb`.
5. **Stream Parser**: Create a stream parser in `lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/stream_parser.rb`.
6. **Error Handler**: Create an error handler in `lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/error_handler.rb`.
7. **Tests**: Add test files in `spec/<new_provider>/`.

### Step 7: Produce the edit map

```json
{
  "files_to_create": [
    "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>.rb",
    "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/request_adapter.rb",
    "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/response_adapter.rb",
    "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/stream_parser.rb",
    "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/error_handler.rb"
  ],
  "files_to_change": [
    "lib/llm.rb",
    "spec/<new_provider>/*_spec.rb"
  ],
  "provider_class": "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>.rb",
  "entry_point": "lib/llm.rb",
  "request_adapter": "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/request_adapter.rb",
  "response_adapter": "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/response_adapter.rb",
  "stream_parser": "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/stream_parser.rb",
  "error_handler": "lib/llm/providers/<new_provider>/error_handler.rb",
  "tests": "spec/<new_provider>/*_spec.rb"
}
```
